notes: Test prompts for the session-log skill. Run with-skill vs baseline (no skill) subagents, then review in the eval viewer. Assertions are mostly programmatic — most can be checked by running scripts/parse_session_log.py over the produced log and grepping for required structure.

evals.name: create-log
evals.prompt: Start a session log for today — I'm refactoring the auth module and want to track decisions as we go.
evals.expected_output: A new file at .claude/session-logs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>.md with all required sections present, a one-line Goal, dated by session start.

assertions.text: A session log file is created under .claude/session-logs/ named YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>.md
parse_session_log.py exits 0 on the created file (conforming)
All 7 required sections are present in the canonical order
A non-empty **Goal:** line is present and reflects the auth refactor
The filename date matches the session start date

evals.name: incremental-update
evals.prompt: We just decided to use JWT over server-side sessions, and I caught a bug where the token expiry check used < instead of <=. Update the session log.
evals.expected_output: A Decisions & trade-offs entry for JWT-vs-sessions (bold heading, alternatives before choice, implication); a Mistakes & corrections entry with the bold Symptom/Root cause/Correct approach triplet; an appended timestamped Update block. Thematic content not duplicated into the Update block.

assertions.text: A Decisions entry has a bold heading and lists the alternative (server-side sessions) before the choice (JWT)
A Mistakes entry uses all three bold labels: **Symptom:** / **Root cause:** / **Correct approach:**
A new ## Update YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM TZ block is appended in the canonical timestamp format
The Update block notes the events but does NOT restate the full decision/mistake prose (no thematic duplication)
parse_session_log.py still exits 0 after the update

evals.name: close-audit
evals.prompt: Last call — wrap up the session log, I'm done for the day.
evals.expected_output: A close-out pass: completed todos struck through (not deleted), a final timestamped Update block, a 'Where to pick up' footer, and a stale-claim audit. No duplicate timestamp blocks.

assertions.text: Completed to-do items are struck through with ~~...~~ rather than deleted
A final ## Update block is appended for the last batch of actions
A non-empty 'Where to pick up' section is present
No duplicate Update timestamp blocks exist
parse_session_log.py exits 0 on the final log
